4 min read
Just now

--

By a Salesforce Architect
Over the past few months, I have been thinking deeply about a question that many Salesforce professionals are asking:
If AI can write code, generate solutions, automate testing, create documentation, and accelerate delivery, what happens to the future of Salesforce Developers and Architects?
A few more questions naturally follow:

• Will businesses reduce technology spending because AI makes delivery faster and cheaper?

• Will companies eventually solve most of their technology challenges and move into a maintenance-only phase?

• Will Salesforce Developers become less relevant as AI writes more code?

• Will Salesforce Architects still be needed when AI can design solutions?
After more than a decade designing and delivering Salesforce enterprise solutions, I see the future differently.
I believe AI will not eliminate Salesforce professionals.
It will redefine what makes them valuable.
— -
Technology Has Always Evolved
The technology industry has experienced several major transformations.
We moved from:

• On-premise applications to cloud platforms

• Traditional development to low-code/no-code solutions

• Manual deployments to DevOps automation

• Custom-built applications to platform ecosystems
Each innovation changed how technology professionals worked.
But businesses did not stop investing in technology.
Instead, expectations increased.
Companies wanted faster delivery, better customer experiences, more automation, and greater innovation.
AI will be another major shift — but not the end of technology careers.
— -
Businesses Will Never Stop Changing
One of the biggest misconceptions is that businesses will eventually “finish” technology.
In reality, businesses never stand still.
Organizations continuously evolve because of:

• New customer expectations

• Market competition

• Regulatory changes

• Expansion into new regions

• New products and services

• Mergers and acquisitions
Every business change creates new technology challenges.
A Salesforce implementation may be completed today, but tomorrow the business may need:

• New customer journeys

• AI-powered service experiences

• New integrations

• Better analytics

• Data modernization

• Automation of additional processes
Technology transformation is not a destination.
It is a continuous journey.
— -
The Future of Salesforce Developers
Let’s acknowledge an important reality.
AI will significantly change Salesforce development.
Many activities that developers perform today will become faster:

• Apex code generation

• Lightning Web Component creation

• Test class generation

• Documentation

• Debugging assistance

• Basic automation

• Repetitive enhancements
A task that takes eight hours today may take one hour tomorrow.
But the role of the developer will evolve.
The future Salesforce Developer will move from:
”Someone who writes code”
to:
”Someone who designs, validates, and delivers intelligent solutions.”
The best developers will focus more on:

• Understanding business problems

• Selecting the right Salesforce capabilities

• Designing scalable solutions

• Ensuring quality and security

• Reviewing AI-generated solutions
The ability to write code will remain important.
But knowing why and when to write code will become even more valuable.
— -
The Future of Salesforce Architects
For architects, the change is different.
AI can help generate:

• Solution designs

• Technical documentation

• Integration patterns

• Code examples

• Architecture recommendations
But enterprise architecture involves much more than producing technical output.
Architects must answer complex questions:

• What solution best supports business strategy?

• Should we customize Salesforce or use standard capabilities?

• How should multiple enterprise systems integrate?

• How do we design for scale and resilience?

• How do we balance cost, security, and maintainability?
Architecture is about decision-making.
It is about understanding the business, managing complexity, and creating a vision.
AI can support architects.
It cannot replace accountability and leadership.
— -
The Biggest Change: From Implementation to Innovation
Historically, much of technology effort has been spent on implementation.
AI will reduce that effort.
But this creates an opportunity.
Instead of spending most of our time:

• Writing repetitive code

• Creating documentation manually

• Building standard components
We can spend more time:

• Understanding business challenges

• Designing better customer experiences

• Creating innovative solutions

• Improving existing platforms
AI allows technology professionals to move higher up the value chain.
— -
The New Salesforce Team Model
The traditional delivery model may change.
Today:

• Salesforce Architect

• Multiple Developers

• QA Engineers

• Business Analysts
Future:

• Salesforce Architect guiding AI-assisted delivery

• Fewer but highly skilled Salesforce Engineers

• Specialists focused on data, integration, security, and AI

• Business experts driving innovation
The team may become smaller.
But each person will have a larger impact.
— -
What Skills Will Matter Most?
For Salesforce Developers:
Focus on:
✅ Salesforce Architecture fundamentals
✅ Integration patterns
✅ Data modeling
✅ Security and governance
✅ AI-assisted development
✅ Business process understanding
For Salesforce Architects:
Focus on:
✅ Enterprise architecture
✅ AI solution architecture
✅ Strategic thinking
✅ Business consulting
✅ Cloud ecosystems
✅ Leadership and communication
The winners will not be those who compete with AI.
They will be those who learn how to amplify their capabilities with AI.
— -
Final Thoughts
AI will undoubtedly change Salesforce careers.
Some repetitive tasks will disappear.
Some traditional responsibilities will evolve.
But businesses will continue to transform, customers will continue to expect better experiences, and technology challenges will continue to emerge.
The future Salesforce Developer will not be the person who writes the most code.
The future Salesforce Architect will not be the person who creates the most documents.
The future belongs to professionals who can combine:
Technology + Business Understanding + AI Capability + Leadership
AI will not replace Salesforce Developers and Architects.
But Salesforce professionals who embrace AI will redefine what is possible.
